fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Louisiana Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Louisiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity louisiana, how to transfer dietitian license to louisiana, dietitian compact louisiana, louisiana dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Louisiana Board of Examiners in Dietetics and Nutrition (LBEDN)
board.mailing_address:
18435 Highland Road, Suite B
Baton Rouge, LA 70809
board.physical_address:
18435 Highland Road, Suite B
Baton Rouge, LA 70809

quick_facts.fee_breakdown: ~$100 application fee (exact amount set by LBEDN; fingerprint fees additional)
quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Annual (by June 30) + 15 CE hours per year

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian-Nutritionist (LDN)
license_types.description: The mandatory credential to use the titles 'dietitian,' 'dietician,' or 'nutritionist' and to practice dietetics or provide nutrition care services in Louisiana. Title protection is strictly enforced.

steps.title: Meet Education Requirements
steps.description: Complete a minimum of a baccalaureate degree from a U.S. regionally accredited college or university with a major course of study in human nutrition, dietetics, food and nutrition, or an equivalent major course of study as determined by the board.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Practice
steps.description: Complete a supervised practice program accredited by the Accreditation Council for Education in Nutrition and Dietetics (ACEND).

steps.title: Pass the Registration Examination
steps.description: Pass the Registration Examination for Dietitians administered by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

documents.detail: Proof of current, active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
From an ACEND-accredited program showing degree completion.
Documentation of 1,200+ hours of ACEND-accredited supervised practice.
Fingerprint-based background check as mandated by 2025 legislation.

provisional_text: Louisiana does not currently offer a provisional or temporary dietitian-nutritionist license. Applicants must complete all requirements, including CDR registration and background check, before licensure is granted.
reciprocity_text: Louisiana does not have a formal reciprocity agreement for out-of-state dietitians. Applicants licensed in other states must apply for a new Louisiana LDN through the standard process. Louisiana is actively monitoring the Dietitian Licensure Compact initiative, which could enable interstate practice if adopted.

faqs.question: How much does a Louisiana dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application fee is approximately $100 (set by LBEDN; contact the Board for the current exact amount). Annual renewal submitted by June 30 is $83. Late renewal (July 1 – August 31) increases to $163. Fingerprint background check fees are additional.

faqs.question: What continuing education does Louisiana require?
faqs.answer: Louisiana requires a minimum of 15 continuing education hours annually in dietetics and nutrition, submitted with your renewal. Alternatively, you may provide a copy of your current CDR identification card to satisfy the CE requirement.

faqs.question: Does Louisiana enforce title protection for dietitians?
faqs.answer: Yes. Louisiana law mandates that only individuals with a valid LDN license may use the titles 'dietitian,' 'dietician,' or 'nutritionist' — or practice dietetics/nutrition care services. Exemptions exist for certain licensed healthcare professionals performing nutrition work incidental to their scope.

faqs.question: Does Louisiana have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Louisiana. To work in Louisiana, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Louisiana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Louisiana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: Louisiana does not have a formal reciprocity agreement for out-of-state dietitians. Applicants licensed in other states must apply for a new Louisiana LDN through the standard process. Louisiana is actively monitoring the Dietitian Licensure Compact initiative, which could enable interstate practice if adopted.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Louisiana
hero.dek: Louisiana Dietitian license requires completing educational requirements, passing the Registration Examination for Dietitians, and completing supervised practice.
